Query: Marks a plugin so the loader picks it up. Does not load here
The plugins that are currently
Analyzes the content of a node and returns a semantic summary and extracted concepts

Applies the given changed files to the cached compilation (reading their current on-disk content) and returns the updated compilation. A changed file that no longer exists is removed; a new one is added; an existing one has its tree replace
Batch-loads the edges touching all hit nodes in a single query and assembles the final list, preserving the input ordering
s the embedding document for under the given source mode

Builds a Roslyn compilation over the.cs files under (skipping bin/obj), or null when there are none. Shared by the full pass and the incremental reconcile (drift): the compilation needs ALL sources to resolve symbols, but the caller can the
Builds a compilation from already-parsed syntax trees (used by the incremental compilation cache)
Merges the non-null fields of into (the current appsettings.Local.json, or null/empty) and returns the new pretty-printed JSON. Pure — no IO
Maps each supported file extension to the parsers that handle it
Builds the grounding prompt that asks the model to hypothesise the link, and to be explicit that it is a guess

Builds the grounded RAG prompt: per-node citation labels, an abstention instruction, and a security framing that the context is untrusted data (not commands). Shared by the blocking and streaming paths

Builds a compact signature line for a node from its stored properties (no body): a method/ constructor as modifiers returnType Name(params), a property as modifiers type Name, and a type as modifiers kind Name
Reports whether a single file's graph representation is in sync with its on-disk content (see), without modifying the graph
95% confidence half-width (normal approximation) so a small golden set reads as a range, not a false-pre sion point estimate
Walks the given (each resolved against the whole) and collects the semantic edges they originate. Shared by the full link and the scoped relink
Extracts the simple type name(s) from a, recursing through nullable/array/pointer/tuple wrappers and generic type arguments. Predefined types (int, string, …) and var / void are ignored

Collects the distinct simple names of all types referenced within a type declaration (base types, field/property types, method return & parameter types, local declarations, object creations, and generic type arguments), excluding the declar
Compiles the demo plugin source into a real.dll on disk (test setup only, not runtime)
Exponential backoff: 30s, 60s, 120s, … capped at
Constructor counterpart of (overloads share the type's name, so only arity discriminates)
Marks a plugin so it is no longer loaded
The distinct names of the type-definition nodes in
Resolves the given (simple) names to C# definition nodes (Class/Interface/Record/Struct/Enum), keyed by name — the basis for reference resolvers (e.g. a config type string → its declaring class node)
Deletes all nodes and associated edges that originate from the specified file path. Used during incremental re-indexing to remove stale data before re-parsing

Deletes all nodes and associated edges for many file paths in a single transaction. Preferred over looping when clearing a large changeset (first index, branch switch, bulk re-scan), where per-file transactions dominate the cost
Deletes edges of the given that ORIGINATE from any node in (outgoing edges). Used to clear a referencer's stale outgoing edges before a scoped relink, without re-parsing that file
Compares the on-disk working tree under against the graph and reports drift, WITHOUT modifying the graph. Uses the same SHA256 content hashes as the incremental scan

Shared body for references at depth 1 (incoming = used_by, outgoing = uses): resolves the symbol, keeps the edges incident to it in the requested direction, and renders them grouped by relationship. is a format string with {0}=name and {1}=

All edges of a given across the whole graph — the basis for structural rules that reason over a relationship globally (e.g. Helix layer-dependency checks over the C# coupling edges). Returns dangling edges too; resolve endpoints via
Packs a float embedding into a little-endian byte blob, or returns null when absent
Releases an index-scan slot previously claimed via
Hashes unless it is empty or already hashed (idempotent migration helper)
Enumerates the files under that match the parser extensions and are not excluded by. Shared by the full scan and drift detection so both see the exact same candidate set
Executes the post-processing phase, mapping cross-technology edges and Helix architecture modules
Reads the.cs files under (skipping bin/obj), builds a compilation, and links the semantic edges into
Executes the tool and returns a complete JSON-RPC response string (success or error), built via
Executes a non-query SQL statement against the given connection
Executes a scalar SQL query and returns the result cast to
Generates the explanation via (both nodes supplied as grounding context) and returns it prefixed with
F2 — Template inheritance. The __Base template field holds a pipe-separated list of base template GUIDs; emits item --INHERITS_FROM--> baseTemplate edges

Extracts the distinct set of changed file paths (added/modified/removed) from a GitHub push payload's commits[]. Returns an empty list when the payload has none (the caller then falls back to a full scan). Malformed payloads yield an empty
Detects relative file links in the Markdown content and creates REFERENCES edges from the current file to each referenced path

F1 — Presentation graph. Parses the Sitecore layout XML stored in __Renderings / __Final Renderings and emits, per rendering instance: item --HAS_RENDERING(placeholder,uid)--> renderingDefinitionItem item --USES_DATASOURCE(rendering,placeho
Splits the content by Markdown header lines and creates a MarkdownSection node for each section, along with a CONTAINS edge from the file node to each section
The simple (unqualified) type name of a syntax, or null for forms we don't name-match (predefined keywords, arrays, tuples, var)
Extracts a ZIP, rejecting any entry whose path escapes the target directory (zip-slip)
Logs the full exception server-side but returns only a generic message to the client, so internal paths/stack details are never leaked over the API
The declaring file path encoded in a C# node id (everything before the first "::"), or null
Returns the tool with the given name, or null if none is registered

Returns the shortest path from to as an ordered list (first element is the source, with a null relation), or null if the target is not reachable within. When the two ids are equal, a single-step path is returned

Resolves the registered project that contains the given directory (deepest/longest path match), or null if none matches. Used to derive context purely from the caller's working directory, independent of the shared, mutable ActiveProjectName
Returns the first non-empty line of that mentions (trimmed, capped at 160 chars), or null — a grep-like usage snippet for find_usages
Constant-time string comparison to prevent timing side-channels on key/secret checks
Node id for a (non-overloadable) member — e.g. a property — of a type
Node id for a type declaration in
Expands a @/<relative> handle back to a real node id; other values pass through unchanged
Kind-aware embedding. Default implementation ignores and delegates to, so existing implementations keep working unchanged; backends that benefit from task prefixes override this
Synthesizes a natural language answer to the user's query based on the provided graph context
Returns every edge in the whole graph. Used by global topology analyses (centrality, community detection) that need the full edge set at once, rather than the per-node or per-relationship views
Retrieves all File node paths currently stored in the graph
Retrieves all nodes from the graph store
The supported clients for the current OS, with per-OS config locations
Returns the stored content hash for each of the given File node IDs (paths) that exist, keyed by node ID. Used for fast incremental-scan change detection without loading content

Resolves the given type names to their definition nodes (Class/Interface/Record/Struct/Enum), keyed by name (a name may map to several same-named definitions across namespaces/files). Lets a scoped relink resolve only the names a file refer
Reads diagnostics, optionally filtered by minimum severity and/or code, highest severity first
Retrieves all edges where both source and target are in the given set of node IDs

Returns every edge of a given relationship type across the whole graph. Used by global structural analyses (e.g. Helix layer-dependency checks over the C# coupling relationships) that reason over a relationship as a whole rather than around
Executes a grouped COUNT query and returns the results as a dictionary
Parses the Helix layer and module from file or Sitecore paths

Returns the edges directly incident to a single node — those where it is the source or the target — together with the nodes on the other end of each edge, keyed by id. Cheaper and more precise than a 1-hop when only a node's direct dependen
The node with the given id, or null if none exists (the basis for unresolved-reference checks)
Retrieves a single node by its identifier, or null if it does not exist

Returns the node-id scheme version this graph was built under (see). A value below the current version means the graph holds ids in an outdated format and a full re-index is recommended. Unstamped legacy graphs read 0
Retrieves all nodes whose equals (the symbols declared in that file). Used by the scoped relink after a single-file re-index
Retrieves nodes filtered by a set of types
Retrieves nodes that are pending semantic analysis
Returns up to nodes that carry an embedding vector (the vector populated), for embedding-based analyses such as surprising-connection detection. A non-positive limit means all
Returns the cached compilation for the directory, building it once if absent. May be null when the directory has no.cs files
Resolves the filesystem root of the requested (or context) project, for path shortening

Returns the distinct file paths that reference any of the given type names (via the reverse index maintained from referencedTypes). Used by drift Layer 2: when a file renames/removes a type, only its referencers need their incoming edges re
Retrieves, in a single query, all edges touching any of the specified node IDs, grouped per node ID. Replaces the previous per-node N+1 lookup
The schema object advertised in tools/list (name + description + inputSchema)
The schemas to advertise in tools/list, filtered by each tool's availability gate
Returns aggregate statistics about the current state of the knowledge graph
Resolves the storage provider for the current request's tenant, taken from the X-Project-Name header (set authoritatively by), falling back to the active project when absent

Returns an initialized storage provider for the given project (or the active project when is null/empty), without blocking the calling thread during first-time schema initialization. Providers are cached per project, so only the first call
Generates a subgraph by expanding outward from the specified seed nodes, by the given number of hops along edges in either direction

Looks up a user by their (plaintext) API token. Tokens are stored hashed, so the presented token is hashed and compared constant-time. Use in auth-critical paths (identical here, kept for call-site clarity)
Looks up a user by their presented (plaintext) API token using a constant-time hash comparison. Use this for every authentication code path
All distinct normalised GUIDs found in a field value (e.g. a pipe-separated multilist)

Prints a ready-to-paste AGENTS.md / CLAUDE.md section so an AI assistant reflexively uses the Shonkor graph (and its edit-validation loop) instead of grepping. Written to stdout — the user decides where to put it (e.g. `shonkor agents >> AG
Plugin lifecycle CLI: install a ZIP (inert), then explicitly activate it. Resolves the workspace like the MCP server (SHONKOR_WORKSPACE, else walk up to the registry marker)
Returns the lowercase hex SHA-256 of (64 chars)

Bounds to chars while keeping BOTH ends of a large symbol (TICKET-105): the head carries the signature/opening, the tail carries return/cleanup logic — so a query targeting either end can still match, instead of only the head being embedded
Matches Markdown header lines (e.g., # Title, ## Section, ### Subsection). Captures the header level (number of # characters) and the header text
The edges incident to a node (where it is source or target), with the other-end nodes keyed by id
Initializes the storage backend, creating required schemas, collections, and indexes if they do not already exist

Installs a plugin from a ZIP package: validates the plugin.json manifest and host-API compatibility, extracts the package into {workspace}/plugins/{id}/, and records it as — INERT. The plugin runs nothing until
Drops the cached compilation for a directory (e.g. after a full scan rebuilt the whole graph)
Whether this tool should be advertised/usable for the given context. Defaults to always available; capability-gated tools (e.g. semantic search) override this
True when the exception indicates the HTTP backend is unreachable or timed out (connection refused, DNS failure, request timeout), as opposed to a per-node logic error
The single source of truth for "this node IS the symbol" (case-insensitive name equality)
Heuristically determines whether a file is binary by scanning the first few KB for a NUL byte. Text source files virtually never contain NUL; most binary formats do within their header
Uses a naming convention heuristic to determine if a base type name refers to an interface. Interface names conventionally start with 'I' followed by an uppercase letter
True when the request originates from the loopback interface (local dashboard)
Whether the effective project opts into semantic indexing and a compilation cache is wired
Parses + upserts the parser nodes for each file, then runs the semantic linker over them
All registered plugins (any state)

Loads every active plugin in the workspace. Failures are isolated: a plugin that won't load is marked in the registry and skipped, so one bad plugin never blocks the others. The returned result must be disposed once the parsers are done
True when already looks like a SHA-256 hex digest
Heuristic for related_tests: whether a file path looks like a test file across common ecosystems (xUnit/NUnit *.Tests, Go _test, Python test_, JS.spec. /.test. / __tests__)
Collapses each community into a super-node: internal edges become self-loops, inter-community edges become weighted super-edges. Total edge weight (and thus m) is preserved
One Louvain level: greedily moves each node to the neighbouring community with the largest positive modularity gain until stable; returns a canonicalized community id per node
Builds a plugin ZIP with the given manifest JSON and a (fake) entry assembly
Records an activation failure (used by the loader when an active plugin won't load)
Matches entries in the OXID metadata 'extend' array. Captures the core class (key) and the module class (value)

Returns the declaration's source offset when the enclosing type declares more than one method with the same name and arity (a same-arity overload group), so their node ids can be made distinct; otherwise null (the common, non-overloaded cas
Resolves the Helix (layer, module) a node belongs to from its file path, or null if outside any layer
All nodes of a given
Canonicalises a Sitecore GUID to lowercase, dashed, brace-less form (matches the Unicorn parser)
Opens a fresh connection for a single operation. The caller owns and disposes it

Returns the declaration's source offset when has a same-kind, same-arity overload sibling in its containing type — mirroring the parser's Method so node and edge ids match. Returns null for non-overloaded methods (stable name#arity id)
Parses a "major.minor" API version, tolerating a missing or malformed minor (treated as 0)
Parses the given file content and extracts graph nodes and edges
Detects PHP class declarations with extends clauses and creates OxidModule nodes with EXTENDS edges to the base class
Parses the JavaScript content and extracts nodes from the program body, creating IMPORTS edges from the component to each import source
Parses the OXID metadata.php file to extract the 'extend' array, which maps core OXID classes to their module-level extensions. Creates EXTENDS edges from the module class to the core class
Parses a PHP file to extract class declarations and, for metadata files, the 'extend' array mapping core classes to module extensions
Parses Smarty template files to detect [{block name="..."}] declarations. Creates a node for the template file and OVERRIDES_BLOCK edges for each block
Whether is admitted by an optional max-uncertainty filter (null = admit all)
Matches PHP class declarations with optional extends clause. Captures the class name and the base class name

Global kill-switch for the assembly-plugin system. The real trust gate is now per-plugin activation (installing a plugin runs nothing), so this defaults to ON; set Security:EnablePlugins=false to hard-disable loading every plugin regardless
Older single-argument entrypoint — runs with no user-configured external prefixes

Enriches a batch of nodes with bounded parallelism: each node is summarized, its summary embedded, and the result persisted. The analyze→embed pair for one node stays sequential (the embedding needs the summary), but separate nodes run conc
Processes one batch per project. Returns true when the semantic backend appears unreachable (so the caller can trip the circuit breaker); false on normal completion

A compact, lower-cased tag for an edge's provenance tier ( [extracted] / [inferred] / [ambiguous]), appended to edge lines so an agent sees whether a relationship is a proven fact or a heuristic guess — Shonkor's core trust signal
Effective current values (config value or the same defaults the services use)
Reads the raw request body and verifies GitHub's HMAC-SHA256 signature (X-Hub-Signature-256) against the configured secret. Returns the raw bytes on success
Reads a double tool argument tolerantly (JSON number or numeric string), else
Reads a from the current row of a data reader
Reads an integer tool argument tolerantly: accepts a JSON number (int or float) or a numeric string, returning for null/missing/non-numeric input instead of throwing
Reads a from the current row, mapping dedicated columns to typed properties and the JSON metadata blob back into
Reads the edge's provenance tier tolerantly: a query that does not select the Provenance column (or a legacy NULL) maps to, preserving the prior semantics

Reads the optional provenance filter argument and returns the maximum uncertainty tier to admit (edges with a tier at or below it pass): extracted → only proven edges; inferred → proven + heuristic, excluding ambiguous; all / ambiguous /mis
Reads the target symbol for symbol-oriented tools, accepting query as a lenient alias of symbol

Drift Layer 3 (reconcile-from-drift): detects drift against the working tree and re-indexes ONLY the changed/new/deleted files surgically (each via, which does the Layer 1+2 scoped relink), instead of a whole-tree rescan. Catches out-of-ban

Drift Layer 4 (git-aware / explicit-set reconcile): re-indexes a KNOWN set of changed paths (e.g. from git diff --name-only or a webhook push payload) surgically via — without hashing the whole tree. Relative paths are resolved against
Shared implementation for the record tool: creates a typed knowledge node, links it to the supplied via, persists both, and returns the JSON-RPC tool response built by
The host's reference assemblies (TPA list), used as compilation references without a build
